title: Speaking – Part 2: Long Turn
description: Each candidate compares two of three photographs and answers two questions about them. You have about one minute. Then your partner answers a brief question.

tasks.instructions: Compare two of the photographs and say what challenge the people might be facing and how they might be feeling.
tasks.mainQuestion: What challenge might the people be facing, and how might they be feeling?
tasks.followUpQuestion: Which situation would you find most challenging?

images.label: a
images.description: A young Asian woman in an airport terminal looking at departure screens, surrounded by busy travelers with suitcases.

images.label: b
images.description: An elderly couple unpacking boxes in a new apartment, filled with scattered moving supplies and an unfurnished living room.

images.label: c
images.description: A group of teenagers in a summer camp, setting up tents in a wooded area under a cloudy sky.

tasks.instructions: Compare two of the photographs and say what decision the people might be making and what factors they might be considering.
tasks.mainQuestion: What decision might the people be making, and what factors might they be considering?
tasks.followUpQuestion: Which decision would be hardest for you to make?

images.label: d
images.description: A middle-aged businessman on a video call in a home office, with a toddler playing on the floor beside him.

images.label: e
images.description: A young mother in a grocery store looking at nutrition labels with a sleeping baby in a stroller next to her.

images.label: f
images.description: A college student with a laptop at a crowded cafe, surrounded by textbooks, appearing focused and slightly stressed.
